title: cytisus austriacus subsp. noeanus
intro: Cytisus austriacus subsp. noeanus, also known as Greek broom, is a flowering plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to Greece. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids and saponins. Cytisus austriacus subsp. noeanus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
